key_insight: A unified constitutive modeling and optimization framework enables predictive design of hard-magnetic soft materials by coupling effective shear-modulus relations with structural, magnetic, and remanence design variables.
engineering_relevance: This supports the design of soft robotic and adaptive structures that deform contact-free under magnetic fields with better control over material layout and actuation response.
method: Unified 7 effective shear-modulus relations with 3 strain-energy densities to form 21 constitutive models
Compared model predictions for actuation problems to identify the most suitable material law
Built joint optimization over structural density, particle volume fraction, and remanent magnetization direction
evidence_signal: Experimental stress-strain data were used to screen the constitutive models, and the Mooney relation gave the best overall agreement in the reported comparisons.
watch_item: The actuation response can depend strongly on the chosen effective shear-modulus relation when magnetic material overlaps highly deforming regions, so constitutive-model selection remains important.
paper_title: Model-Informed Joint Material-Structural Optimization of Hard-Magnetic Soft Materials
